question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a crafted TopicMessageFilter with a manipulated startTime set to 0 (epoch) to force the service to replay the entire historical message stream for a topic, causing the subscriber to receive tampered ordering of transactions that misrepresents the true submission sequence?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: missingMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the gap recovery logic in missingMessages() by triggering a scenario where current.getSequenceNumber() - last.getSequenceNumber() - 1 yields a large numMissingMessages value, causing the service to issue an unbounded retriever query that floods the stream with attacker-influenced message content?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: onNext()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the onNext() method to throw an IllegalStateException by injecting an out-of-order message into the live stream via topicListener, thereby terminating the subscriber's stream and causing them to miss legitimately submitted transactions?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: isNext()] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with the sequence number of a submitted transaction so that isNext() returns true for a forged message, allowing a fabricated message to be inserted into the stream as if it were a legitimately submitted transaction?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user open an unlimited number of concurrent subscriptions via subscribeTopic() with no rate limiting, causing subscriberCount to grow unboundedly and exhausting server-side reactive scheduler threads, resulting in denial of service that prevents legitimate transaction submissions from being observed?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: topicExists()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the checkTopicExists=false configuration path in topicExists() to subscribe to a non-existent topic ID, receiving a synthetic Entity with no real backing, and then observe gap-recovery queries that leak information about real submitted transactions on adjacent topic IDs?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: missingMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a scenario where the live listener delivers a message with a very high sequenceNumber relative to last, causing numMissingMessages to be computed as a very large positive value, triggering a retriever query with limit=numMissingMessages that causes excessive database load and distorts the apparent transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: incomingMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the limit recalculation in incomingMessages() where limit = filter.getLimit() - topicContext.getCount().get() to underflow to a negative value if count exceeds the original limit due to a race condition, causing the newFilter to be built with a negative limit that bypasses retrieval constraints?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user set filter.getEndTime() to a value only slightly greater than filter.getStartTime() to cause the flux to immediately complete via takeWhile(), silently dropping all submitted transactions in that window and making the subscriber believe no transactions were submitted?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: isComplete()] Can an unprivileged external user set filter.getEndTime() to a value less than the TopicContext.startTime (DomainUtils.now() at construction) to cause isComplete() to immediately return true, preventing the safety check and live stream from ever running and causing all submitted transactions after startTime to be silently dropped?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a filter with limit=Long.MAX_VALUE to cause flux.take(filter.getLimit()) to hold the stream open indefinitely, consuming server resources and preventing the stream from completing even after all submitted transactions have been delivered?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: missingMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the safety check path (current == null) in missingMessages() repeatedly by maintaining a subscription where topicContext.isComplete() is always false and no live messages arrive, causing the service to issue infinite gap-recovery queries that enumerate all submitted transactions from startTime?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the filter(t -> last == null || t.getSequenceNumber() > last.getSequenceNumber()) deduplication in subscribeTopic() to receive duplicate submitted transactions when the historical and live streams overlap, causing the subscriber to process the same transaction twice and corrupt application state?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: pastEndTime()] Can an unprivileged external user set endTime to a far-future value and exploit the pastEndTime() polling loop (RepeatSpec with Long.MAX_VALUE repeats) to keep the stream alive indefinitely, consuming bounded elastic scheduler threads and starving other subscribers from receiving submitted transactions?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: topicExists()] Can an unprivileged external user enumerate valid topic IDs by observing the difference in error responses between EntityNotFoundException (topic not found) and IllegalArgumentException (not a valid topic), allowing them to map out which entity IDs correspond to topics and target specific transaction streams for tampering?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: onNext()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a race condition in onNext() by exploiting the non-atomic check-then-act pattern where isNext() is checked and then last.set() is called separately, allowing a concurrent subscriber on the same TopicContext to interleave and corrupt the last reference, causing legitimate submitted transactions to be skipped?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: missingMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the concatWithValues(current) call in missingMessages() to receive a message that was already delivered by the historical retriever, causing the same submitted transaction to appear twice in the stream and leading to double-processing by the subscriber?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a filter with startTime=0 and no endTime or limit to create a permanent subscription that streams all historical and future submitted transactions for a topic, effectively wiretapping the entire transaction history without any access control check beyond topic existence?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: incomingMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the startTime recalculation in incomingMessages() (last.getConsensusTimestamp() + 1) to cause the live listener to be initialized with a startTime that skips over submitted transactions that arrived between the historical retrieval and the live subscription setup?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Flux.merge(safetyCheck, live) combination in subscribeTopic() to receive messages from both the safety check and the live stream simultaneously, causing the same submitted transaction to be delivered twice if the safety check and live stream overlap in their time windows?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: isComplete()] Can an unprivileged external user manipulate the endTime in the filter to be exactly at the boundary of grpcProperties.getEndTimeInterval() past the current time, causing isComplete() to oscillate between true and false and creating a race condition where submitted transactions near the boundary are inconsistently included or excluded?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: missingMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the newFilter built in missingMessages() to have endTime=current.getConsensusTimestamp() and startTime=last.getConsensusTimestamp()+1 with limit=numMissingMessages, and if the database returns more messages than numMissingMessages due to a race, cause extra submitted transactions to be injected into the stream out of order?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that topicExists() runs concurrently with the flux construction in subscribeTopic() to subscribe to a topic that is deleted between the existence check and the actual message retrieval, receiving messages from a topic that no longer exists?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user set a very large limit value combined with startTime=0 to force the historical retriever to load the entire topic message history into memory, causing an out-of-memory condition that crashes the gRPC service and prevents all other subscribers from receiving submitted transactions?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: incomingMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the filter.toBuilder().limit(limit).startTime(startTime).build() call in incomingMessages() where limit=0 (when filter has no limit) to cause the newFilter passed to topicListener.listen() to have limit=0, which hasLimit() interprets as no limit, potentially streaming all future submitted transactions?
